I want: to sign up with full name / email / password
So that: I can create an account and use the tool
Acceptance Criteria: Valid email + policy-compliant password creates an account and redirects to login; confirm-password must match; duplicate email rejected with a clear message; password stored only as a salted hash
OWASP / Security Notes: A07 Auth: strong password policy; A02 Crypto: bcrypt/Argon2 + per-user salt, never plaintext; A03: parameterized insert; A04: rate-limit signup

I want: to sign in with email and password
So that: I can securely reach my projects
Acceptance Criteria: Valid credentials issue a session/JWT and land on workspace; invalid credentials return a generic message; account throttles/locks after N failed attempts
OWASP / Security Notes: A07: generic error to block enumeration; brute-force throttling; A02: HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite cookie or secured JWT; A09: log auth failures

I want: to reset my password via an emailed link
So that: I can regain access when I forget it
Acceptance Criteria: Registered email triggers a time-limited single-use token; identical confirmation shown for known/unknown emails; valid token sets a new password then is invalidated
OWASP / Security Notes: A07: single-use expiring token; A01: bind token to user; no email-existence disclosure; A02: hash reset token at rest

I want: to log out
So that: my session cannot be reused
Acceptance Criteria: Logout invalidates token/cookie server-side; protected routes then redirect to login
OWASP / Security Notes: A07: server-side session invalidation; clear cookies; A05: secure session config

I want: to edit name / phone / company / designation / bio
So that: my account and proposals show accurate details
Acceptance Criteria: Saving in Settings > Profile persists values and reloads next visit; email change requires re-verification
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: users edit only their own profile; A03: validate/sanitize all fields; cap lengths

I want: to upload a profile picture
So that: my account is personalized
Acceptance Criteria: Only allowed type/size accepted and ProfileImageUrl stored; oversized or non-image files rejected
OWASP / Security Notes: A03/A08: validate content-type + magic bytes, cap size; store outside webroot; strip EXIF; AV scan

I want: to create a project with name / client / description
So that: I can group my estimation work
Acceptance Criteria: Submitting a name creates a project persisted to my user and selects it in the sidebar; name required
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: project owned by and scoped to creating user; A03: sanitize name/description against stored XSS

I want: to see my projects and open one
So that: I can switch between workspaces
Acceptance Criteria: On load only my projects appear; selecting one loads its conversations and views
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: server returns only the authenticated user's projects (prevent IDOR)

I want: to rename or delete a project
So that: I can keep my workspace tidy
Acceptance Criteria: Editing/deleting a project I own persists; delete cascades to conversations/requirements/modules/estimations after a confirmation prompt
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check before mutate/delete; confirm destructive action; A09: audit deletions

I want: to set status (Draft / In Progress / Completed)
So that: I know where each estimation stands
Acceptance Criteria: Changing status persists it and shows it in listings
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check; validate status against allowed set

I want: to describe my project in chat and have requirements gathered
So that: I avoid long forms
Acceptance Criteria: Sending a description runs the requirements agent, replies conversationally, and persists each turn; response returns within the latency budget
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate conversation_id/message; treat model output as untrusted; A10 SSRF: outbound only to configured LLM; A04: rate-limit chat

I want: to be told what information is still missing
So that: I know what to provide before estimation
Acceptance Criteria: Incomplete input yields isMatched=false and a populated missingRequirements list; projectDetails stays null until complete
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: server never trusts client-supplied isMatched; persistence derived from agent result

I want: to have finalized requirements saved
So that: I can revisit and estimate from them
Acceptance Criteria: When the agent returns isMatched=true with projectDetails, a single requirements row is upserted per conversation and rendered as a structured card
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: requirement tied to owned conversation/project; A08: upsert keyed by conversation to avoid duplicates

I want: to reopen a conversation and see prior messages
So that: I can continue where I left off
Acceptance Criteria: Opening a conversation returns messages ordered by creation with role and kind so structured cards re-render
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: only owner can read a conversation's history (no IDOR on conversationId)

I want: to attach documents to seed requirements
So that: the assistant can extract requirements from my files
Acceptance Criteria: Drag-drop or picker accepts allowed types/size; extracted text feeds the requirements agent; unsupported/oversized files rejected
OWASP / Security Notes: A03/A08: content-type + size validation, sandbox parsing; A10: no external fetch from file content

I want: to send one message and have it routed to the right agent
So that: I do not have to pick tools manually
Acceptance Criteria: Orchestrator routes each message to analyze_requirements or estimate_modules (or a plain reply), persists the turn and any structured result, and returns a uniform response with tool_invoked/kind/data
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate inputs; A09: log tool routing decisions; server derives persistence, not client

I want: to see which estimation stage is running
So that: I understand progress across the pipeline
Acceptance Criteria: The pipeline bar shows each agent as pending/active/completed and advances as stages finish
OWASP / Security Notes: A09: reflect only server-confirmed state to avoid misleading progress

I want: to break my completed requirements into software modules
So that: I can see the building blocks of my project
Acceptance Criteria: Given completed requirements, running estimation returns a module list (name, description, is_required, dependencies), replaces any prior set for the conversation, and persists it
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate conversation_id + projectDetails present; A01: modules scoped to owned conversation

I want: to review add edit or remove modules
So that: I can correct the AI's decomposition
Acceptance Criteria: The Modules view lists persisted modules; I can add/edit/remove entries and set complexity; changes persist
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: mutate only modules of owned project; A03: validate names and complexity enum

I want: to set a complexity level per module
So that: estimation reflects relative effort
Acceptance Criteria: Each module accepts Low/Medium/High; the value persists and feeds effort estimation
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate complexity against allowed set

I want: to auto-generate user stories for each module
So that: I get a work breakdown without writing them all
Acceptance Criteria: For a module, the agent produces stories with title, description, priority and story points, each linked to the module and project, and persisted
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate module ownership; treat generated text as untrusted before render; A04: rate-limit generation

I want: to edit reprioritize or delete generated stories
So that: the backlog matches reality
Acceptance Criteria: I can edit fields, change priority, set story points, and delete stories I own; changes persist
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check on each story; A03: validate priority/points ranges

I want: to capture acceptance criteria
So that: scope is testable and unambiguous
Acceptance Criteria: I can add/edit/remove acceptance criteria linked to a module or story; they persist and appear in exports
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check; A03: sanitize criteria text

I want: to decompose stories into subtasks with estimated hours
So that: I get granular effort figures
Acceptance Criteria: For a story, the agent generates subtasks (name, description, type, complexity, estimated hours) linked to the story; totals roll up
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate story ownership; bound numeric hours; treat AI output as untrusted

I want: to adjust subtask hours and complexity
So that: estimates reflect my judgement
Acceptance Criteria: I can edit estimated hours/type/complexity of my subtasks; totals recompute and persist
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check; A03: validate numeric ranges to prevent overflow/negative

I want: to get an AI complexity score and reasons per module
So that: I understand risk drivers behind estimates
Acceptance Criteria: Each module gets a complexity level, numeric score, and reasons, persisted and shown alongside the module
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: validate module ownership; bound score range; sanitize reasons

Story Title: Estimate hours and confidence per module
I want: to see estimated hours and a confidence score per module
So that: I can gauge reliability of each estimate
Acceptance Criteria: Each module shows estimated hours and a confidence score derived from complexity/subtasks; values persist
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: bound numeric outputs; A01: scoped to owned project

I want: to see total hours/days/weeks with a risk buffer and confidence
So that: I can quote the whole project
Acceptance Criteria: Totals aggregate module/subtask hours, apply a risk-buffer, and yield final hours plus overall confidence; a single project-estimation snapshot persists and recomputes when inputs change
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: scoped to owned project; A08: recompute server-side, never trust client totals; A09: log recomputes

I want: to record assumptions with a category
So that: estimates are transparent about what they rely on
Acceptance Criteria: I can add/edit/remove assumptions (description + category) linked to the project; they persist and appear in the proposal
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check; A03: sanitize description against stored XSS

I want: to have the assistant suggest likely assumptions
So that: I do not miss common caveats
Acceptance Criteria: The agent proposes assumptions from the requirements; I can accept, edit, or dismiss each; accepted ones persist
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: treat AI suggestions as untrusted until accepted; A04: rate-limit generation

I want: to record risks with severity and mitigation
So that: stakeholders see delivery risk
Acceptance Criteria: I can add/edit/remove risks (description, severity, mitigation) linked to the project; they persist and appear in the proposal
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check; A03: validate severity enum; sanitize text

I want: to have the assistant surface risks
So that: I get a fuller risk register
Acceptance Criteria: The agent proposes risks with suggested mitigations from requirements/modules; I can accept/edit/dismiss; accepted persist
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: treat AI output as untrusted; A04: rate-limit generation

I want: to auto-generate a proposal from all collected data
So that: I can deliver a client-ready document
Acceptance Criteria: The agent compiles objective, scope, modules, stories, estimates, assumptions and risks into a formatted proposal stored per project and opened in the editor
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: sanitize/escape all interpolated data to prevent HTML/stored XSS in the doc; A01: scoped to owned project

I want: to refine the generated proposal
So that: the final document matches my voice
Acceptance Criteria: The TipTap editor supports headings, tables, lists, alignment, links; edits persist as a proposal version
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: sanitize pasted HTML; restrict link protocols (no javascript:) ; A05: safe editor config

I want: to export the proposal (e.g. PDF/DOCX)
So that: I can share it outside the tool
Acceptance Criteria: Exporting produces a file matching the on-screen layout; export reflects the latest saved content
OWASP / Security Notes: A03: sanitize before render; A08: generate server-side from stored content where possible for integrity

I want: to keep and reopen prior proposal versions
So that: I can compare or roll back
Acceptance Criteria: Each generation/save creates a dated version I can reopen; the latest is shown by default
OWASP / Security Notes: A01: ownership check on version access; A09: record who generated each version

I want: to switch between light and dark themes
So that: the UI suits my environment
Acceptance Criteria: Toggling theme updates the UI immediately and persists the preference across reloads
OWASP / Security Notes: A05: no security impact; store preference client-side only

I want: to use the tool on varied screens with assistive tech
So that: the product is usable and compliant
Acceptance Criteria: Core flows are keyboard-navigable, have visible focus and ARIA labels, and meet WCAG AA contrast; layout adapts to smaller widths
OWASP / Security Notes: Accessibility per WCAG 2.1 AA; full validation needs manual AT testing and expert review

I want: to keep API keys out of source
So that: the LLM key is not leaked
Acceptance Criteria: The OpenRouter key is read from environment/user-secrets/secret store, never hardcoded; startup fails clearly if the key is absent in non-dev
OWASP / Security Notes: A05 Misconfig / A02: remove hardcoded fallback key and rotate it; load from secret store; never log the key

I want: to lock down origins and enforce HTTPS
So that: the API is not abused cross-origin
Acceptance Criteria: CORS allows only configured origins per environment; production enforces HTTPS/HSTS; methods/headers scoped to what is needed
OWASP / Security Notes: A05 Misconfig: no wildcard origins with credentials; A02: TLS + HSTS; scope allowed methods/headers

I want: to require auth and ownership checks on every endpoint
So that: users cannot read or change others' data
Acceptance Criteria: Every project/conversation/history/modules endpoint requires a valid session and verifies the resource belongs to the caller; unauthorized returns 401/403 without data leakage
OWASP / Security Notes: A01 Broken Access Control / IDOR: enforce ownership on conversationId, projectId and every child resource; A07: require auth

I want: to validate all inputs and throttle abuse
So that: the API resists injection and DoS
Acceptance Criteria: All request DTOs are validated (type, length, allowed values); chat/generation endpoints are rate-limited per user; oversized payloads rejected
OWASP / Security Notes: A03 Injection; A04 Insecure Design: rate-limit LLM-backed endpoints to control cost/DoS; bound payload sizes

I want: to return generic errors without internal details
So that: attackers gain no diagnostic info
Acceptance Criteria: Unhandled errors return a generic message and correlation id; stack traces/exception messages are not sent to clients; details go to server logs only
OWASP / Security Notes: A05/A09: stop leaking ex.Message; log server-side with correlation id

I want: to log security-relevant events
So that: I can detect and investigate incidents
Acceptance Criteria: Auth events, access-control denials, and estimation/proposal generation are logged with actor, action, timestamp; logs exclude secrets and PII where possible
OWASP / Security Notes: A09 Logging & Monitoring: structured, tamper-evident logs; no secrets/PII in logs

I want: to constrain what the model output can trigger
So that: malicious inputs cannot drive unsafe actions
Acceptance Criteria: Model output is only ever treated as data (rendered/persisted), never executed; tool routing is server-controlled; document/file content cannot inject instructions that change server behavior
OWASP / Security Notes: A03/A04 + LLM01: never execute model output; sanitize before render; isolate untrusted document text from system prompts

I want: to pin and scan dependencies
So that: known-vulnerable or typosquatted packages are avoided
Acceptance Criteria: Backend and frontend dependencies are pinned; CI runs a vulnerability scan; new packages are reviewed before adoption
OWASP / Security Notes: A06 Vulnerable & Outdated Components: pin versions, run SCA (dotnet list package --vulnerable / npm audit)
